sources.text: The Antikythera mechanism is an ancient Greek analog device used to predict astronomical positions and eclipses.
It was discovered in 1901 in a shipwreck near the Greek island of Antikythera and contains at least thirty bronze gears.

answer: The Antikythera mechanism is an ancient Greek device to predict astronomical positions and eclipses. It was discovered in 1901 in a shipwreck and contains thirty bronze gears. The mechanism was personally built by the mathematician Archimedes.
